content:
Given the undirected dynamic graph with the edges [(0, 1, 0), (1, 2, 1), (0, 2, 2), (2, 6, 1), (5, 7, 1), (5, 6, 5), (1, 7, 6), (1, 8, 6), (1, 4, 7), (1, 2, 8), (3, 4, 8), (6, 8, 9), (0, 2, 14), (5, 9, 15), (7, 8, 17)], we need to find when node 7 and node 5 are first connected.

We will scan each edge and collect the time t whenever u and v match the two queried nodes (in either order).

1. (0, 1, 0) - Time 0
2. (1, 2, 1) - Time 1
3. (0, 2, 2) - Time 2
4. (2, 6, 1) - Time 3
5. (5, 7, 1) - Time 4
6. (5, 6, 5) - Time 5
7. (1, 7, 6) - Time 6
8. (1, 8, 6) - Time 7
9. (1, 4, 7) - Time 8
10. (1, 2, 8) - Time 9
11. (3, 4, 8) - Time 10
12. (6, 8, 9) - Time 11
13. (0, 2, 14) - Time 12
14. (5, 9, 15) - Time 13
15. (7, 8, 17) - Time 14

Now we need to find the first time when node 7 and node 5 are connected. We can do this by scanning the times from 0 to 14 and checking if node 7 and node 5 are connected at any point.

At time 4, node 5 is connected to node 7, so we have:

Answer: 4
